items.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
items.summary: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg texted Elon Musk to offer help with DOGE
items.summary: While the relationship was once thorny enough that Musk challenged Zuckerberg to a cagefight, things seem to have warmed up by the early days of the second Trump administration.

items.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
items.summary: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

items.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
items.summary: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

items.title: These iPad apps will make you wish you had more free time
items.summary: We’ve compiled a list of some of the best iPad apps for creativity that are available on the App Store.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
items.summary: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

items.title: What will power the grid in 2035? The race is wide open
items.summary: Fusion, fission, and even natural gas are appeared tied in the race to deliver new power to the grid in the early 2030s.

items.title: Let’s take a look at the retro tech making a comeback
items.summary: Boomboxes, instant cameras, and even landlines are making a comeback. Here are the coolest retro-inspired devices available.

items.title: From Moon hotels to cattle herding: 8 startups investors chased at YC Demo Day
items.summary: We polled nearly a dozen VCs to find out which W26 startups are the sought after in the batch.

items.title: Whoop’s fitness band is cool. Can it stay cool as the company grows?
items.summary: Whoop founder Will Ahmed has spent 14 years building a health wearable beloved by elite athletes, and is now racing Oura — and the FDA, and the limits of consumer medicine — to turn it into something that could one day save your life.

items.title: Physical Intelligence is reportedly in talks to raise $1 billion, again
items.summary: The deal would effectively double the company's $5.6 billion valuation in just four months.

items.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
items.summary: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

items.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
items.summary: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.

items.title: Waymo’s skyrocketing ridership in one chart
items.summary: Waymo's weekly paid robotaxi trips have increased tenfold in less than two years.

items.title: European Commission confirms cyberattack after hackers claim data breach
items.summary: The European Union's top executive body has confirmed a cyberattack after hackers reportedly stole reams of data from the European Commission's cloud storage.

items.title: Aetherflux reportedly raising Series B at $2 billion valuation
items.summary: Index Ventures is said to be leading a $250 million to $350 million round.

items.title: Rivian gets another $1B from Volkswagen
items.summary: The two companies have been collaborating on a joint venture that would see Rivian tech go in VW's EVs.

items.title: Iranian hackers claim breach of FBI director Kash Patel’s personal email account
items.summary: Handala, a pro-Iranian hacking group allegedly working for Iran’s government, published emails it said were taken from the Gmail account of FBI director Kash Patel.

items.title: Apple says no one using Lockdown Mode has been hacked with spyware
items.summary: The tech giant's claim that it has not seen any successful spyware attacks targeting Apple devices with Lockdown Mode enabled comes amid a leak of hacking tools targeting users running devices with older software.

items.title: OpenAI shuts down Sora while Meta gets shut out in court
items.summary: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]
